changes.motivation: On first-time runs of `pf sso add`, the `authentik_aws_sso/module.yaml` file does not yet exist. `getInputsFromAuthentikAWSSSOModule` called `readYAMLFile` without `throwOnMissing: false`, causing it to throw instead of returning an empty object, which aborted the setup flow before any prompts were shown.
changes.summary: Added `throwOnMissing: false` to the `readYAMLFile` call inside `getInputsFromAuthentikAWSSSOModule` in `packages/cli/src/commands/sso/add/setupFederatedAuth.ts`. This makes the function return `undefined` (and thus `{}` via the nullish coalesce) when the module file is absent, allowing first-time setup to proceed correctly.

problems: The `readYAMLFile` utility defaults `throwOnMissing` to `true`, but callers that want to optionally read a config file that may not exist must remember to explicitly pass `throwOnMissing: false`. This is a footgun: the silent omission of that flag in `getInputsFromAuthentikAWSSSOModule` caused a hard crash on every first-time run. The function signature or its documentation should make the default more visible, or a separate `readOptionalYAMLFile` wrapper should be provided to make optional reads the path of least resistance.
